name: Brex API
what_it_does: Brex's REST API gives programmatic access to corporate cards, budgets, expenses, reimbursements, and vendor payments — designed for finance automation in tech startups and growth companies.
use_cases: Issuing virtual cards with custom limits and restrictions for autonomous agent procurement
Syncing Brex transactions and receipts to accounting systems (QuickBooks, NetSuite, Xero)
Automating expense approval workflows from policy-based agent evaluation
Managing team budgets programmatically — create, update, and monitor spend limits
Triggering vendor payments and ACH transfers from financial automation workflows
not_for: Non-US companies (Brex is primarily US market)
Consumer personal finance (B2B corporate cards only)
Companies needing physical cards before credit underwriting approval
best_when: Your startup or growth company uses Brex and needs agents to issue cards, track spend, or sync financial data automatically without manual finance team intervention.
avoid_when: You're outside the US or need mature enterprise expense features — Concur or Coupa are more established for large enterprise.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 with client credentials for server-to-server. User-level OAuth for acting on behalf of employees. Scopes: transactions.readonly, cards, expenses, budgets, payments. Token expiry with refresh flow.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Brex platform is free for businesses — earns on interchange
pricing.paid_tiers: Brex Premium and Enterprise for advanced features; no API fees
pricing.notes: Like Ramp, Brex is free because they earn interchange. API access included. Must apply for a Brex business account with credit approval.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; fair-use limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard REST errors with error_code and message. OpenAPI spec available for SDK generation. Webhook events for spend require HMAC signature validation. Paginated lists use cursor-based approach.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency-Key header supported on payment and card creation endpoints. Agents should always use idempotency keys for financial mutations.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth token scopes must be requested at auth time — no dynamic scope escalation without re-authorization
Card budget association is required — agents must create a budget before issuing cards against it
Real-time transaction webhooks may have 2-5 minute delay from card swipe to webhook delivery
Brex does credit checks — API access and card limits depend on Brex's credit decision, not just API credentials
Expense policies and approval workflows are configured in Brex UI, not API — agents must respect pre-configured policy IDs

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 with scoped access. PCI-DSS Level 1 compliant. SOC 2 Type II certified. Webhook HMAC for payload validation. Card PAN masked in API responses — never returned in full.
